subject: Warp Crashes During BoxNet Training
body:
Hi Dmitry,

I am working on setting up a new boxnet model, and I got the following warp 
crash after training a model, not being happy with it, picking new 
particles, and training for the second time:

8/7/2019 10:26:10 AM:
> System.IO.IOException: The process cannot access the file 
> 'C:\Users\baradb\AppData\Roaming\Warp 1.0.7 
> beta2\boxnet2models/GenentechNet2Fibers_20190807/saved_model.pb' because it 
> is being used by another process.
>    at System.IO.__Error.WinIOError(Int32 errorCode, String maybeFullPath)
>    at System.IO.File.InternalCopy(String sourceFileName, String 
> destFileName, Boolean overwrite, Boolean checkHost)
>    at Warp.BoxNet2.Export(String newModelDir)
>    at 
> Warp.Controls.BoxNetTrain.<>c__DisplayClass16_0.<<ButtonRetrain2_OnClick>b__0>d.MoveNext()
> --- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown 
> ---
>    at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
>    at 
> System.Runtime.CompilerServices.TaskAwaiter.HandleNonSuccessAndDebuggerNotification(Task 
> task)
>    at Warp.Controls.BoxNetTrain.<ButtonRetrain2_OnClick>d__16.MoveNext()
> --- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown 
> ---
>    at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
>    at System.Windows.Threading.ExceptionWrapper.InternalRealCall(Delegate 
> callback, Object args, Int32 numArgs)
>    at System.Windows.Threading.ExceptionWrapper.TryCatchWhen(Object 
> source, Delegate callback, Object args, Int32 numArgs, Delegate 
> catchHandler)


I got another crash when I re-opened warp and tried to train again:

>
> 8/7/2019 10:31:06 AM:
> TensorFlow.TFException: Attempting to use uninitialized value 
> batch_normalization_52/gamma
> [[Node: batch_normalization_52/gamma/read = Identity[T=DT_FLOAT, 
> _class=["loc:@train_momentum/update_batch_normalization_52/gamma/ApplyMomentum"], 
> _device="/job:localhost/replica:0/task:0/device:GPU:0"](batch_normalization_52/gamma)]]
> [[Node: train_momentum/_15 = _Recv[_start_time=0, client_terminated=false, 
> recv_device="/job:localhost/replica:0/task:0/device:CPU:0", 
> send_device="/job:localhost/replica:0/task:0/device:GPU:0", 
> send_device_incarnation=1, tensor_name="edge_20976_train_momentum", 
> tensor_type=DT_INT64, 
> _device="/job:localhost/replica:0/task:0/device:CPU:0"]()]]
>    at TensorFlow.TFStatus.CheckMaybeRaise(TFStatus incomingStatus, Boolean 
> last)
>    at TensorFlow.TFSession.Run(TFOutput[] inputs, TFTensor[] inputValues, 
> TFOutput[] outputs, TFOperation[] targetOpers, TFBuffer runMetadata, 
> TFBuffer runOptions, TFStatus status)
>    at TensorFlow.TFSession.Runner.Run(TFStatus status)
>    at Warp.BoxNet2.Train(Single[] data, Single[] labels, Single[] weights, 
> Single learningRate, Int32 threadID, Int64[]& argmax, Single[]& probability)
>    at Warp.BoxNet2..ctor(String modelDir, Int32 deviceID, Int32 nThreads, 
> Int32 batchSize, Boolean forTraining)
>    at 
> Warp.Controls.BoxNetTrain.<>c__DisplayClass16_0.<<ButtonRetrain2_OnClick>b__0>d.MoveNext()
> --- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown 
> ---
>    at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
>    at 
> System.Runtime.CompilerServices.TaskAwaiter.HandleNonSuccessAndDebuggerNotification(Task 
> task)
>    at Warp.Controls.BoxNetTrain.<ButtonRetrain2_OnClick>d__16.MoveNext()
> --- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown 
> ---
>    at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
>    at System.Windows.Threading.ExceptionWrapper.InternalRealCall(Delegate 
> callback, Object args, Int32 numArgs)
>    at System.Windows.Threading.ExceptionWrapper.TryCatchWhen(Object 
> source, Delegate callback, Object args, Int32 numArgs, Delegate 
> catchHandler)


These seem to be two very different issues, but since I am using Warp 1.0.7 
beta 3 (which is labeled in the logs as beta 2, but I checked is indeed 
beta 3) I thought I should report both!

Thanks,
Ben
